query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Bestow', 'To lay up in store, deposit for safe keeping, stow, place', 'The author hoped the new policy would bestow tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bestrew', 'To strew or scatter about, throw or drop here and there', 'Critics argued that the plan might bestrew the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bestride', 'To be astride something', 'Over time, the community learned to bestride its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bethink', 'To think about, to recollect', 'Over time, the community learned to bethink its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Betide', 'To happen to', 'Over time, the community learned to betide its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Betim', 'Before the usual or expected time', 'The essay highlights the betim of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Betroth', 'To promise to give in marriage', 'The author hoped the new policy would betroth tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bevel', 'To give a canted edge to a surface', 'The author hoped the new policy would bevel tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bewilder', 'To cause someone to become perplexed and confused', 'Over time, the community learned to bewilder its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bias', 'Showing prejudice or favoritism toward a particular person, group, or idea', 'The passage presents a bias portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bibliography', 'A section of a written work with citations to all references', 'The author hoped the new policy would bibliography tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bibliomania', 'The obsession of owning valuable books', 'Scholars noted a striking bibliomania between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bibliophile', 'One who loves books', 'Her bibliophile became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bibulous', 'Very absorbent', 'What seemed bibulous at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bicameral', 'Pertaining to, two separate legislative chambers or houses', 'The essay highlights the bicameral of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bicker', 'To quarrel in a tiresome, insulting manner', 'The author hoped the new policy would bicker tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bide', '(chiefly dialectal) to bear, to endure, to tolerate', 'Over time, the community learned to bide its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Biennial', 'Happening every two years', 'Scholars noted a striking biennial between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bier', 'A litter to transport the corpse of a dead person', 'Critics argued that the plan might bier the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bifurcate', 'To divide or fork into two channels or branches', 'Over time, the community learned to bifurcate its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bigamy', 'The state of having two spouses simultaneously', 'Scholars noted a striking bigamy between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bight', 'A corner, bend, or angle', 'Scholars noted a striking bight between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bigot', 'A person utterly intolerant of any differing opinion', 'Her bigot became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bilateral', 'Having two sides', 'What seemed bilateral at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bilge', 'The widest circumference or belly of a cask', 'Scholars noted a striking bilge between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bilingual', 'Fluent in two languages', 'Her bilingual became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bilious', 'Peevish, irritable', 'The reviewer described the tone as bilious, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bilk', 'To obtain money fraudulently', 'Critics argued that the plan might bilk the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Billow', 'Present participle of billow', 'Her billow became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Biography', 'A written account of another person''s life', 'Scholars noted a striking biography between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Biome', 'A large ecological region characterized by distinct plant and animal life', '“The Amazon rainforest is one of the most diverse biomes on Earth.”', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bip', 'An animal, or being that goes about on two feet or legs', 'Her bip became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Birthright', 'Something owed since birth, due to inheritance', 'Critics argued that the plan might birthright the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bitterness', 'The quality of having a bitter taste', 'Scholars noted a striking bitterness between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bivouac', 'To set up camp', 'The author hoped the new policy would bivouac tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bizarre', 'Strangely unconventional in style or appearance', 'Scholars noted a striking bizarre between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Blanch', 'To grow or become white', 'Critics argued that the plan might blanch the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bland', 'Mild, soft, gentle', 'The essay highlights the bland of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Blandish', 'To persuade someone by using flattery, to cajole', 'Critics argued that the plan might blandish the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Blare', 'To make a loud sound', 'The author hoped the new policy would blare tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Blasé', 'Uninterested because of frequent exposure or indulgence', 'The essay highlights the blasé of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Blaspheme', 'To speak against god or religious doctrine', 'Critics argued that the plan might blaspheme the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Blatant', 'Done openly and unashamedly', 'The reviewer described the tone as blatant, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Blaze', 'A bright flame or fire', 'Scholars noted a striking blaze between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Blazon', 'To describe a coat of arms', 'The author hoped the new policy would blazon tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Bleak', 'Bare, desolate', 'The essay highlights the bleak of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Blemish', 'Spoil the appearance of', 'Scholars noted a striking blemish between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Blend', 'Mix, coalesce, fuse, merge, mingle', 'The essay highlights the blend of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Blender', 'A kitchen appliance used for mixing, puréeing, or emulsifying food and other substances', 'Scholars noted a striking blender between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Blight', 'To suffer blight', 'The author hoped the new policy would blight tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
